landing.no_landings: No landings recorded yet — fly your first PIREP and it will show up here.
landing.confirm_delete: Really remove this landing from the history?
landing.heading: Heading
landing.no_plan_hint: No plan values available — no SimBrief OFP was loaded for this flight. On your next flight, watch the activity log for a "SimBrief OFP fetched" entry at start; if it's missing, the bid had no plan or the fetch failed silently.
landing.coach_title: What to focus on next time

info.landing_rate: Vertical speed (V/S) at the moment of touchdown, in feet per minute. Negative = sinking. Boeing/Airbus FCOM thresholds: clean up to -240 fpm; hard-landing inspection at -600 fpm.
info.g_force: Peak G load in the 1.5-second window around touchdown. 1.0 G is smooth, above 1.4 G is clearly noticeable, above 1.7 G triggers a hard-landing inspection (Boeing 737 FCOM).
info.bounces: How many times the aircraft climbed back above 35 ft AGL after the first touchdown and re-landed (BeatMyLanding algorithm). 0 = clean set; each bounce drops the score.
info.stability: Standard deviation (σ) of vertical speed over the last ~10 minutes of approach. Low = smooth, constant flying. < 100 fpm σ is 'very stable'; > 300 fpm σ warrants a stabilized-approach review.
info.rollout: Percent of available runway length used from touchdown until clearing the runway (high-speed exit below 40 kt or > 30° heading deviation). < 70 % is normal; > 85 % is tight, especially on wet runway.
info.fuel: Deviation of actual trip burn from the SimBrief plan. Positive = burned more, negative = less. ±5 % means clean performance calc; > ±20 % suggests wrong cruise altitude or unaccounted wind.
info.score_section: Six sub-scores rated 0-100 each. Every card shows value, points, and a coloured bar (green/yellow/red). The weakest category becomes the focus of the coach tip below.
info.runway_section: Visualisation of your touchdown on the runway. The cyan dot marks the touchdown point; the green band shows the rollout distance until you cleared the runway (high-speed exit or stop). Values below: total length, centerline offset, distance past threshold, runway used as a percentage.
info.fuel_section: Block = fuel at chocks-off; TO Fuel = at takeoff position; LDG Fuel = at touchdown; TOW = takeoff weight (empty + payload + TO fuel); LDW = landing weight. Plan values come from the SimBrief OFP if available. Plan-vs-actual delta drives the fuel-efficiency score.

tip.smooth_touchdown: Exactly right — flare timing was on point and the descent rate bled off cleanly.
tip.firm_but_clean: Solid landing. For a softer touchdown, start the flare 1-2 seconds earlier.
tip.above_target: More flare and time the thrust reduction slightly later — don't push down.
tip.hard_landing: Control approach descent (target -700 fpm in final), then a clean flare around 30 ft AGL.
tip.very_hard: Classic hard-landing pattern. Review stabilized-approach criteria, flare timing and pitch attitude.
tip.severe_inspection: Structurally critical — would be a real-world inspection event. Prioritise approach stability.
tip.smooth_g: Excellent touchdown mechanics. Keep doing this.
tip.comfortable_g: Good. Pitch transition into the flare was clean.
tip.noticeable_g: More flare and softer set — raise pitch slightly later.
tip.firm_g: G spike too high — delay pitch increase in the flare and set down more gently.
tip.hard_g: Touched down hard. Reduce thrust earlier and use a smoother flare.
tip.severe_g: G-critical — review the entire approach profile.
tip.clean_set: Perfect — set down once and stayed put.
tip.one_bounce: Hold pitch (or raise slightly) at TD instead of pushing forward, then set down softly.
tip.two_bounces: Bouncing comes from over-flaring or excess sink rate. Fly a more stable approach.
tip.many_bounces: Multiple bounces indicate an unstable TD profile. Consider a go-around if unsure.
tip.very_stable: Textbook stable approach — V/S and bank barely moved.
tip.stable: Stable approach. Keep on the constant glideslope.
tip.average_stability: OK, but reduce pitch and V/S oscillations — small corrections, not big ones.
tip.unstable_approach: Approach was choppy. Check stabilized-approach criteria: config, speed, V/S, track by 1000 ft AAL.
tip.very_unstable: Very unstable approach — would be a real-world go-around candidate. Set up the approach earlier.
tip.excellent_stop: Excellent — short rollout, good control. No reverser overuse.
tip.long_rollout: Slightly long rollout. Consider auto-brake or earlier reverser application.
tip.very_long_rollout: Very long rollout. Check auto-brake setting or apply reversers earlier.
tip.marginal_runway: Runway margin was tight — risky on wet runway. Use a higher auto-brake setting.
tip.on_plan: Fuel exactly on plan — clean performance computation.
tip.near_plan: Very close to plan, all green.
tip.off_plan: Drift from OFP. Check cruise Mach and altitude vs plan.
tip.very_off_plan: Significant plan deviation. Check cost index, cruise altitude or wind impact.
tip.way_off_plan: Way off plan — possibly wrong OFP or different routing.
tip.fallback: Solid landing. The score breakdown shows where there's still room to grow.

about.tagline: Modern ACARS for phpVMS 7 — MSFS 2020/2024 + X-Plane 11/12
about.purpose_body: A modern open-source ACARS client — Tauri-based, Rust + React, cross-platform. Captures telemetry from the sim, scores landings with industry-grounded thresholds, correlates touchdowns to runway-centerline precision, and ships clean PIREPs to phpVMS.
about.acknowledgements_intro: These projects were studied, measured, or referenced extensively:
about.thresholds_intro: Smooth/Acceptable/Firm/Hard/Severe boundaries come from real pilot literature:

cockpit.empty_hint: Head to Briefing to start a booked flight or adopt an in-progress PIREP.
cockpit.pirep_filed_detail: {{callsign}} · {{dpt}} → {{arr}} — flight submitted to phpVMS. It's now in the Landing tab and on the phpVMS website.

loadsheet.title: Live loadsheet
loadsheet.label: Loadsheet
loadsheet.hint_ready: ✓ Ready for pushback — values close to plan
loadsheet.hint_overfueled: 💡 +{{extra}} kg above plan — expect higher burn

briefing.active_flight_hint: {{callsign}} is in progress — switch to the Cockpit tab to manage it.

activity_log.title: ACARS activity log
activity_log.empty: No events yet. Logins, flight starts, phase transitions and PIREP filings will show up here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.simulator_hint: Pick your simulator. AeroACARS connects automatically as soon as the sim is running.
settings.auto_file_hint: Files the PIREP automatically once the flight reaches the "Arrived" phase (at gate, engines off, parking brake set). The manual "End flight" button remains available for cases where validation fails.
settings.debug_mode_hint: Shows the full telemetry grid (position, altitude, speeds, attitude, G-load) on the dashboard. Not needed for normal use.
settings.auto_delete_logs_hint: On the next app start, flight log files older than 30 days will be deleted. The current recording is never affected.
settings.delete_all_logs_confirm: Delete every saved flight log right now? This includes the currently active flight (if any) — its file gets recreated on the next event. This step cannot be undone.

behaviour.minimize_to_tray_label: Minimize to system tray on close
behaviour.minimize_to_tray_hint:
Instead of quitting, AeroACARS is minimized to the system tray (macOS: menubar). Click the tray icon to bring the window back. Use the tray menu → "Quit" to actually exit the app.

Recommended during active flights: AeroACARS keeps running in the background and pushes heartbeat + position updates to phpVMS.

release_notes.error_offline: Couldn't load release notes from GitHub — are you offline?
release_notes.error_no_release: No release notes available on GitHub for this version yet.

divert.body_alternate: You landed at {{actual}} (your planned alternate) instead of {{planned}} (~{{distance}} nmi from destination).
divert.body_nearest: You landed at {{actual}} instead of the planned {{planned}} (~{{distance}} nmi from destination).
divert.body_unknown: You landed ~{{distance}} nmi away from {{planned}}, but we couldn't match the actual airport against our database. Please enter the ICAO manually.
divert.manual_modal_hint: Pick from the nearest airports or enter an ICAO code manually.

phpvms_status.title: phpVMS API
phpvms_status.no_active_flight: No active flight — telemetry to the phpVMS site starts as soon as you begin a flight (or adopt one in progress).

pmdg_panel.title: PMDG Premium Telemetry
pmdg_panel.inactive_hint: Load a PMDG aircraft (737 / 777) and live cockpit data straight from the PMDG SDK will show up here.
pmdg_panel.sdk_disabled_title: PMDG detected, but SDK not enabled yet
pmdg_panel.sdk_disabled_explanation: For AeroACARS to read FMA, MCP values, V-speeds and FMC data straight from the cockpit, one line needs to go into the PMDG options file. Takes 30 seconds:
pmdg_panel.step_add_lines: Add these two lines at the end:
pmdg_panel.step_save_restart: Save, restart MSFS, reload the flight — done.
pmdg_panel.active_hint: Premium cockpit data flowing — FMA, MCP, V-speeds and FMC values appear in the activity log and get recorded in the PIREP.
pmdg_panel.waiting: Subscription registered — waiting for first data packet …
pmdg_panel.waiting_for_sim: Waiting for sim connection — SDK status check resumes once MSFS / X-Plane is connected.
pmdg_panel.waiting_for_aircraft: Waiting for aircraft load — SDK status check resumes once the aircraft is fully loaded in MSFS / X-Plane.
pmdg_panel.waiting_init: Subscription registered — giving PMDG {{secs}} s more to initialise …

xplane_premium_panel.title: X-Plane Premium Plugin
xplane_premium_panel.active_hint: Native telemetry is streaming from X-Plane — touchdowns are captured in the exact frame of wheel contact (peak descent VS, pitch-corrected).
xplane_premium_panel.inactive_hint_xp_running: Optional: the AeroACARS plugin provides frame-perfect landing-rate capture. Copy the plugin into X-Plane/Resources/plugins/AeroACARS/ and restart X-Plane.
xplane_premium_panel.inactive_hint_xp_not_running: Will activate as soon as X-Plane runs with the AeroACARS plugin installed. Standard RREF capture works without it.
xplane_premium_panel.bind_error_explanation: Port 52000 is held by another application — most likely a second AeroACARS instance is running. Close it and click "Re-check sim".
xplane_premium_panel.detect_success: X-Plane install found — you can install now.
xplane_premium_panel.detect_failed: Could not auto-detect X-Plane — please enter the path to your X-Plane root folder manually.
xplane_premium_panel.install_success: Plugin installed at {{path}} ({{files}} files). Restart X-Plane and the status will turn "live".

pilot_header.location_tooltip: Location per website ({{airline}}) — where your last PIREP landed. If this looks stale after a flight, hit "Refresh" top-right.
pilot_header.home_tooltip: Home base per website ({{airline}}) — your career anchor.

login.title: Connect to phpVMS
login.description: Enter your phpVMS site URL and API key. The key is stored securely in your operating system's credential store and never written to disk in plaintext.

error.invalid_url: That doesn't look like a valid URL. Use https:// (recommended) or http://.
error.network: Could not reach the server. Check your internet connection and the URL.
error.unauthenticated: The API key was rejected. Please double-check it.
error.forbidden: The server refused the request. Your account may lack the required permissions.
error.not_found: The phpVMS API endpoint was not found at that URL. Is this really a phpVMS 7 site?
error.rate_limited: Too many requests — please try again in a minute.
error.server: The server returned an error. Try again or contact your VA admin.
error.bad_response: The server response could not be understood. The site may not be running phpVMS 7.
error.keyring: Could not access the OS credential store.
error.config_path: Could not determine where to store the configuration.
error.config_read: Could not read the saved configuration.

sim.title: Simulator
sim.connecting_hint: Waiting for the simulator… the adapter will connect automatically once it starts.

diff.title: Switch Detective (snapshot diff)
diff.hint: Take Snapshot A → flip a switch in the cockpit → take Snapshot B. The table lists only the fields that changed.

inspector.title: SimVar / LVar Inspector
inspector.hint: Type a SimVar or LVar name to watch its value live. Useful for figuring out which LVar a cockpit switch maps to.

resume.title: Active flight detected
resume.countdown: Resuming in {{seconds}}s — no position stream is running yet
resume.confirm_cancel: Cancel the active flight? The PIREP will be marked as "cancelled" on phpVMS — it stays visible in the PIREP list. Full deletion only via the phpVMS website.

active_flight.title: Active flight
active_flight.confirm_cancel: Really discard this flight? The PIREP will be marked as "cancelled" on phpVMS — it stays visible in the PIREP list. Full deletion only via the phpVMS website.
active_flight.forget_hint: Drop local flight state without contacting phpVMS — useful if the PIREP is already gone server-side.
active_flight.confirm_forget: Drop local flight state? phpVMS will NOT be contacted. Only use this if 'Discard flight' fails.
active_flight.refresh_ofp_hint: Pull a fresh SimBrief OFP for the running flight — useful if you regenerated the OFP on simbrief.com after starting the flight.
active_flight.refresh_ofp_done: Plan values refreshed from SimBrief. The loadsheet now compares against the new plan.

paused.title: Sim disconnected — flight paused
paused.instructions: The simulator hasn't sent data for more than 30 seconds. Restart the sim, load your aircraft at the position shown below (or a deliberately different one — your call), and restore fuel + ZFW. Then click "Resume flight". No position updates go to phpVMS in the meantime; the PIREP itself stays active.

validation.title: PIREP can't be filed yet
validation.intro: The following required data is missing or implausible — filing now would store a broken flight on phpVMS:

fields.flight_time: Flight time too short (at least 1 minute required)
fields.distance: No distance flown (at least 1 nm required)
fields.position_count: Too few position datapoints (at least 5 required)
fields.fuel: Fuel data missing (block or remaining fuel not captured)
fields.not_arrived: Aircraft is not parked at the destination — engines-shutdown / parking-brake sequence not detected
fields.no_bid: No booking attached — without a bid the PIREP can't be filed as a pure ACARS report
fields.not_at_arrival: You're not at the arrival airport — file as a manual divert with a reason instead

validation.options_title: What do you want to do?
validation.option_manual_hint: Submits the PIREP with source 'manual' — VA admin will review. You can add a reason and a divert airport.
validation.option_cancel_hint: Discards the PIREP entirely on phpVMS.
validation.manual_form_intro: The PIREP will be filed as a manual report tagged 'AeroACARS (manual)' and reviewed by the VA admin.
validation.divert_hint: If you landed somewhere other than the planned arrival ({{planned}}).
validation.reason_placeholder: e.g. Weather at EDDF, diverted to EDDV — or: resume lost the fuel data

bids.title: My booked flights
bids.empty: You don't have any booked flights. Book one on your phpVMS site and click "Refresh" here.
bids.simbrief_unavailable: Plan values could not be loaded
bids.ofp_mismatch_title: SimBrief OFP doesn't match the booking
bids.ofp_mismatch_hint: SimBrief always returns the most recently created OFP. Please create a fresh OFP for this booking on simbrief.com — otherwise the plan values (block fuel, TOW, route) don't match your flight.
bids.no_ofp_title: No SimBrief OFP for this booking
bids.no_ofp_hint: No OFP is linked to this booking yet. Generate one on simbrief.com — the plan values (block fuel, TOW, route, alternate) will appear here once it's there. You can also start without an OFP — there'll just be no plan/actual comparison.

auto_start_skip.title: Auto-start active, but currently not possible
auto_start_skip.engines_on: Engines are running — auto-start only works on a cold-and-dark aircraft. Use 'Start flight' manually.
auto_start_skip.moving: Aircraft is already taxiing — auto-start only fires while parked. Use 'Start flight' manually.
auto_start_skip.airborne: You're airborne — auto-start only works on the ground.

bids.start_disabled_not_on_ground: You must be on the ground to start the flight
bids.start_disabled_no_position: Waiting for sim position … (may take a moment after a sim reload)
bids.sim_position_warning: Sim position not received yet — give it a moment, or hit "Refresh" top-right if the distance shown below looks wrong.
bids.start_disabled_too_far: You are {{distance}} nm from {{airport}} — start the flight at the departure airport

error.not_logged_in: Not signed in — please sign in again.
error.unauthenticated: Session expired — please sign in again.
error.forbidden: You don't have permission to fetch bids.
error.not_found: The bids endpoint was not found on the site.
error.rate_limited: Too many requests — please wait a moment.
error.bad_response: The server response could not be understood.

error.no_sim_snapshot: No simulator data — is the simulator connected?
error.not_on_ground: You must be on the ground to start the flight.
error.not_at_departure: You are not at the departure airport — please taxi to the right airport.
error.missing_airline: This flight has no airline assigned.
error.missing_aircraft: No aircraft for this flight — please prepare a SimBrief OFP first.
error.aircraft_not_available: Aircraft not available — see details below. Common causes: the aircraft is parked at another airport, is currently 'in use' by an open PIREP, or your rank doesn't grant access. Fix in phpVMS admin: cancel orphan PIREPs and verify the aircraft's location.
error.aircraft_mismatch: Aircraft type mismatch — you're in the wrong aircraft for this bid. Load the correct type in the simulator or pick a different bid.
error.phpvms_error: phpVMS rejected the flight — see details.
